tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: A Stranger's Honesty Saves Two Girls' Summer Camp Dreams
stories.summary: A Good Samaritan found $700 in a parking lot and returned it to a family whose daughters' camp plans hung in the balance—one act of integrity changing everything.

stories.headline: Devoted Grannies Hand-Knit Warmth Into Young Hearts
stories.summary: Grannies Nan Hackner, Judy Levin, and Beulah Chatz hand-knitted colorful beanies all summer for Grade 1 students at Orange Grove Primary School, delivering warmth and a lesson in community care.

stories.headline: One Chef's Lasagnas Feed 25 Families and Lift Spirits
stories.summary: A volunteer chef delivers homemade lasagnas to families in need through Lasagna Love, wrapping genuine warmth in every meal shared across her community.

stories.headline: From Wheelchair to Semifinal: Czech Teen's French Open Miracle
stories.summary: Six days after needing a wheelchair, 20-year-old Czech player Jakub Mensik powered into the French Open semifinals, winning his quarterfinal 6-4, 6-3, 7-6.

stories.headline: A Couple's 'Bail Out Boxes' Offer Real Help, No Judgment
stories.summary: Marcus and Steph Griggs created Bail Out Boxes, delivering emergency food and toiletries to families across six towns, driven by Steph's childhood poverty and their daily work in specialist schools.

stories.headline: Two Parakeets Give Their Entire Species a Second Chance
stories.summary: A pair of super-breeder orange-fronted parakeets produced 55 chicks in two years, boosting their critically endangered species' population by over 10 percent.

stories.headline: From 8 to 150: Woodpeckers Return to Restored Forests
stories.summary: Red cockaded woodpecker populations in Virginia grew from just 8 birds to 150 as conservationists restored longleaf pine forests, bringing the endangered species back from the brink.

stories.headline: Right Whales Experience Their Best Year in 17 Years
stories.summary: North Atlantic right whales delivered 23 mother-calf pairs in 2026—their strongest breeding season since 2009—signaling real recovery from the brink of extinction.

stories.headline: Magnetic Microrobots Heal Spinal Cord Injuries in Breakthrough
stories.summary: Zurich researchers guide magnetic microrobots carrying stem cells to repair spinal cord damage, restoring movement in mice—a leap toward healing human paralysis.

stories.headline: Postage-Stamp Ultrasound Pacemaker Heals Hearts Without Surgery
stories.summary: MIT engineers created an ultrasound sticker no bigger than a postage stamp that corrects heart arrhythmias without surgery, safely restoring normal heartbeats in lab and animal tests.

stories.headline: Neighbours Rally Around Seniors in Mapleton's Good Neighbour Project
stories.summary: Volunteers in Mapleton are quietly helping seniors with everyday tasks, proving that community care—one blue box at a time—still lifts us all.

stories.headline: Indian Badminton Duo Claim Singapore Open Through Unshakable Belief
stories.summary: Indian badminton duo Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty won the Singapore Open by staying mentally calm after losing the first game, trusting their process and accepting defeat's possibility.
